roles.title: Senior Product Manager, Google Play App
roles.role_summary: Senior PM for the Google Play App. Requires 8 years PM experience and 3 years of 0-to-1 product launches. Consumer-facing product role at Google's app distribution platform — a massive India surface given Android dominance.
roles.why_good_fit: Google Play is a critical consumer surface in India, especially for Tier 2/3 users — aligning with Jay's deep expertise in Indian mass-market consumer behaviour. The 0-to-1 requirement maps to founding team experience at a music streaming app and startup founding. India is Google Play's largest market by users, so Jay's Indian market intelligence and vernacular user understanding are directly relevant.
roles.how_to_win: Frame experience around understanding Indian consumer app discovery and engagement patterns. Highlight the music streaming app's growth story (content discovery, recommendation, Tier 2/3 user thesis) as analogous to app discovery on Play. Emphasise field research methodology and ability to translate on-ground Indian user insights into product decisions. Address the 8-year experience gap by showcasing breadth across consumer product, monetisation, and growth.
roles.job_description: Senior Product Manager, Google Play App. 8 years PM experience, 3 years 0-to-1 product launches.

roles.title: Senior Product Manager, Android Developer Console
roles.role_summary: Senior PM for Android Developer Console — the tool developers use to publish, manage, and grow their apps on Google Play.
roles.why_good_fit: Jay has 5+ years PM experience (requirement is 5 years) and 2+ years of zero-to-one product building (Resso founding team, travel startup). His experience with app ecosystems (Resso on Android, TikTok) gives him developer-side perspective. His understanding of Indian developers and SMBs could be valuable for Developer Console's India market. However, this is a developer tools/B2B product, not a consumer product — it's a reasonable but not ideal fit. His AI fluency could be relevant for AI-powered developer tools.
roles.how_to_win: Frame your Resso experience from the developer/publisher perspective — you understand what app developers need because you've been one. Highlight your understanding of the Indian developer ecosystem. Emphasize your zero-to-one experience and your ability to build tools that serve diverse user segments (Tier 2/3 developers, not just Silicon Valley). Mention your AI OS as evidence you can envision AI-powered developer tools.
roles.job_description: You'll own product areas within Android Developer Console — the primary tool developers use to publish apps, manage releases, analyze performance, and grow their apps on Google Play. Day-to-day involves defining developer-facing features, working with engineering teams, gathering developer feedback, and shipping tools that improve the developer experience. Success means developers finding it easier and more effective to build and grow apps on Android.

roles.title: Product Manager, Payments Risk
roles.role_summary: PM for payments risk — managing fraud detection, risk assessment, and trust & safety for Google's payment products.
roles.why_good_fit: Jay's SMB over-enforcement work at Meta is an almost perfect analogy — he identified that Meta's global enforcement model was creating false positives that devastated Indian SMBs, then drove policy change and a new agentic resolution system. This is fundamentally a payments risk problem: balancing fraud prevention with legitimate user access. He understands the India-specific nuances of risk (informal businesses, different CRM patterns, voice-led operations) that global risk models miss. The 3-year minimum experience requirement means Jay comes in as a very senior candidate with 7 years.
roles.how_to_win: This is your SMB enforcement story on steroids. Lead with: 'At Meta, I discovered that our global enforcement model was over-blocking legitimate Indian SMBs — I drove the creation of a new product team, an agentic resolution system, and delivered a 2.3% India revenue uplift.' Frame this as a payments risk problem: false positives in enforcement = false positives in fraud detection. Emphasize that you understand the India-specific risk landscape — informal businesses that look suspicious to global models but are perfectly legitimate. Show you can balance risk mitigation with user growth.
roles.job_description: You'll own the risk management product surface for Google's payment products — defining how fraud is detected, how legitimate transactions are protected, and how risk models are tuned. Day-to-day involves working with ML teams on risk models, analyzing fraud patterns, defining policy frameworks, and ensuring the right balance between security and user experience. Success means reducing fraud losses while minimizing false positives that block legitimate users.

roles.title: Product Manager, eWallets
roles.role_summary: PM for Google's eWallet products — digital wallet experiences likely spanning Google Pay and related financial products, with India as a key market.
roles.why_good_fit: Jay's deep understanding of Indian digital payment behaviour — especially among SMBs and informal businesses — is directly relevant. His Meta SMB work (10M businesses impacted, 2.3% revenue uplift) demonstrates he can identify product gaps in how Indian users interact with digital financial tools. His insight that Indian SMBs operate through voice-led, informal digital patterns is exactly the kind of user intelligence an eWallet PM needs. The 5-year experience requirement matches his ~7 years. His zero-to-one experience (Resso founding team, travel startup) shows he can build new product surfaces.
roles.how_to_win: Position yourself as someone who understands how money actually moves in India — not through global product assumptions, but through on-ground observation. Lead with the bakery story and SMB enforcement work. Emphasize that you've seen firsthand how Indian users (especially Tier 2/3) interact with digital financial tools. Highlight your Resso experience to show you can build consumer products from scratch. If the role involves emerging market expansion, lean into your India market intelligence as a competitive advantage.
roles.job_description: You'll own product strategy and execution for Google's eWallet products, likely focused on digital wallet experiences for consumers and merchants. Day-to-day involves defining features, working with engineering and payments infrastructure teams, analyzing transaction data, and driving wallet adoption. Success means growing wallet usage, improving user retention, and expanding the merchant acceptance network.

roles.title: Product Manager, Cloud Database
roles.role_summary: PM for Google Cloud's database products — managing database services for enterprise Cloud customers.
roles.why_good_fit: Jay meets the 5-year PM experience requirement and has zero-to-one building experience. However, this role requires 2 years of database/analytics/big data product experience, which Jay lacks. It's a deeply technical B2B infrastructure role without India market growth or AI-native components. His consumer product and India market strengths don't translate strongly here.
roles.how_to_win: This is a stretch role. If applying, emphasize your technical product intuition and ability to learn domains quickly. Frame your data analysis work at Meta (Unidash, opportunity sizing) as adjacent. Highlight your AI-assisted data analysis capabilities. However, this is likely not the best use of Jay's application energy.
roles.job_description: You'll own Google Cloud database products — managed database services used by enterprise customers for their applications. Day-to-day involves defining database product roadmaps, working with database engineering teams, gathering enterprise customer requirements, and shipping features. Success means growing database service adoption and customer satisfaction.

roles.title: Product Manager, Payments Platform
roles.role_summary: PM for Google's payments platform infrastructure — the underlying systems that power Google Pay and other payment experiences.
roles.why_good_fit: Jay's SMB and payments-adjacent work at Meta (WhatsApp API, Paid Messaging, SMB advertiser conversion) gives him context on payment ecosystems. His 2.3% India revenue uplift from SMB enforcement work shows he understands how platform-level decisions impact business metrics. The 5-year PM requirement matches. However, this is more platform/infrastructure-focused than consumer-facing, which is a slight mismatch with Jay's strongest skills. His India market intelligence remains valuable for a payments platform serving Indian users.
roles.how_to_win: Frame your Meta work on WhatsApp business products (API, Paid Messaging) as payments-adjacent platform experience. Lead with the SMB enforcement story to show you understand how platform decisions cascade to business outcomes. Emphasize your understanding of Indian payment behaviour — informal businesses, voice-led operations, UPI ecosystem dynamics. Position yourself as someone who brings the user perspective to platform decisions.
roles.job_description: You'll own parts of Google's payments platform — the infrastructure layer that powers payment experiences across Google products. Day-to-day involves defining platform capabilities, working with engineering teams on scalability and reliability, and ensuring the platform supports product teams building payment features. Success means a robust, scalable payments platform that enables rapid feature development.

roles.title: Product Manager, Google Play Apps
roles.role_summary: PM for Google Play's apps ecosystem — managing the app discovery, distribution, and developer experience on Android's primary app marketplace.
roles.why_good_fit: Google Play is a massive consumer platform with deep India relevance — India is Android's largest market. Jay's experience scaling Resso on Android (Tier 2/3 India users, ML-driven discovery) maps to app discovery challenges. His understanding of Indian consumer segments (regional users, GenZ, informal businesses) is valuable for Play's India growth. His ByteDance experience with app ecosystem dynamics (TikTok, Resso) gives him platform-level thinking. 5 years PM experience required matches his ~7 years.
roles.how_to_win: Frame your Resso experience as app ecosystem expertise — you built and scaled an app from alpha to India's #1 in its category on Android. You understand app discovery, user acquisition, and retention in the Indian market. Highlight your insight about Tier 2/3 users being underserved by global products — this applies directly to Google Play's India opportunity. Mention your competitive intelligence work (TikTok re-entry strategy) to show you understand platform dynamics. Emphasize your AI fluency as relevant to AI-powered app discovery.
roles.job_description: You'll own a product area within Google Play Apps — the world's largest app marketplace. Day-to-day involves defining features that improve app discovery, developer tools, or user engagement, working with engineering teams, analyzing marketplace data, and driving growth metrics. Success means growing app installs, improving developer satisfaction, and enhancing the user experience of finding and using apps.

roles.title: Product Manager, Google Pay
roles.role_summary: PM for Google Pay — India's leading consumer payments product, serving hundreds of millions of Indian users across UPI, merchant payments, and financial services.
roles.why_good_fit: Google Pay is a quintessential India consumer product — Jay's superpower is translating on-ground Indian user observations into product decisions at scale. His SMB work at Meta (bakery insight → 2.3% India revenue uplift, 10M businesses impacted) directly maps to Google Pay's merchant ecosystem. He understands how Indian SMBs and informal businesses actually transact — voice-led WhatsApp operations, informal digital literacy — which is critical for Google Pay's merchant growth. His Shoppertainment work (commerce + entertainment convergence) shows he thinks about payments in context. 5 years PM experience required matches his ~7 years perfectly.
roles.how_to_win: Lead with the Dahod bakery story — you discovered a massive enforcement problem by talking to a real SMB owner, then drove a 2.3% India revenue uplift. This shows you understand Indian merchants at a visceral level. Frame your SMB growth work at Meta as directly transferable to Google Pay's merchant ecosystem. Highlight your insight that Indian SMBs operate through voice-led WhatsApp — this is the kind of on-ground intelligence Google Pay needs. Mention your Shoppertainment work to show you think about payments as part of a broader commerce ecosystem, not just transactions.
roles.job_description: You'll own a product area within Google Pay, India's massive consumer payments platform. Day-to-day involves defining product strategy for user-facing payment experiences, working with engineering and design to ship features, analyzing user behaviour data, and driving adoption metrics. Success means growing active users, transaction volume, and merchant engagement on the platform.

roles.title: Product Manager, AI Gaming, Google Play
roles.role_summary: PM for AI-powered gaming experiences on Google Play — at the intersection of AI product development and the gaming ecosystem on Android.
roles.why_good_fit: This role sits at the AI + consumer product intersection that Jay targets. His AI fluency (top 5% Metamate user, personal AI OS, 25hrs/week on AI tools) demonstrates he can think about AI product applications deeply. His consumer product experience (Resso, Meta consumer products) shows he understands user engagement and retention. India is the world's largest mobile gaming market — Jay's Tier 2/3 India user expertise is directly relevant. The 5-year PM requirement matches his ~7 years. His zero-to-one experience (Resso founding team) maps to building new AI-powered gaming features.
roles.how_to_win: Lead with your AI fluency story — you built a personal AI operating system on Metamate, automating 80% of tasks, top 5% user globally. Then connect to your consumer product experience: Resso's ML-driven recommendation engine is analogous to AI-powered game discovery. Highlight your understanding of Indian mobile gaming users (Tier 2/3, regional, GenZ). Frame yourself as someone who can bridge AI capabilities with consumer product intuition — not just an engineer, but someone who understands what AI features users actually want.
roles.job_description: You'll own AI-powered gaming features on Google Play — think AI-driven game discovery, personalized recommendations, or AI-enhanced gaming experiences. Day-to-day involves defining AI product strategy for gaming, working with ML and engineering teams, running user research with gamers, and shipping features that grow gaming engagement on Play. Success means leveraging AI to meaningfully improve the gaming experience and drive growth metrics.

roles.title: Product Manager, Google Cloud Business Platform
roles.role_summary: PM for Google Cloud's business platform — enterprise tools and infrastructure for Cloud customers.
roles.why_good_fit: Jay has 5+ years PM experience matching the requirement, and zero-to-one product building experience. His cross-functional leadership at Meta (working with product, policy, data, marketing, engineering teams) translates to Cloud platform PM work. However, this is a B2B/enterprise infrastructure role without a clear India market growth or AI-native component, which are Jay's non-negotiables. His SMB expertise could be relevant if the platform serves SMB Cloud customers.
roles.how_to_win: If applying, emphasize your cross-functional execution skills and zero-to-one building experience (Resso founding team). Frame your SMB work as understanding how businesses adopt technology platforms. Highlight your AI fluency as relevant to Cloud's AI integration strategy. Be honest that your background is more consumer-facing but position the transition as bringing fresh user-centric thinking to enterprise products.
roles.job_description: You'll own product areas within Google Cloud's business platform — tools and infrastructure that enterprise customers use to manage their Cloud deployments. Day-to-day involves defining product requirements, working with engineering teams, gathering customer feedback, and shipping platform features. Success means enterprise customers adopting and getting value from the platform.

roles.title: Product Manager, YouTube Create
roles.role_summary: PM for YouTube Create — a consumer-facing creator tool product within YouTube, focused on enabling video creation and editing experiences for creators.
roles.why_good_fit: Jay's creator economy experience is directly transferable: he scaled Resso from alpha to India's largest music streaming app, led TikTok artist activation (Ghost Music Tab), and worked on creator monetisation research at ByteDance. At Meta, he contributed to Shoppertainment (creator-commerce convergence) and content relevance for regional/long-tail creators. His deep understanding of Indian GenZ creators (GenZ Product Council member) and regional/vernacular users gives him a unique edge for YouTube Create's India-based team. 5 years PM experience required — Jay has ~7 years, perfect seniority match.
roles.how_to_win: Lead with the Resso founding story — zero-to-one creator product, ML-driven recommendations, scaling to beat Spotify in India. Then pivot to Meta's creator growth work and Shoppertainment. Frame yourself as someone who understands what creators in India actually need (not what global teams assume). Emphasize your field research methodology — 20+ AI user interviews, bakery SMB discovery — as evidence you'll bring real creator insights to YouTube Create. Mention your AI OS built on Metamate as proof you can leverage AI tools to accelerate product development.
roles.job_description: You'll own product strategy and execution for YouTube Create, a creator-focused tool that helps YouTubers produce and edit content. Day-to-day involves defining product roadmaps, working with engineering and design teams, running user research with creators, and shipping features that grow creator adoption and content quality. Success means more creators using YouTube Create to produce better content, driving engagement and platform growth.

roles.title: Senior Product Manager, YouTube, Creation and Platform
roles.role_summary: Senior PM for YouTube's Creation and Platform team, focused on consumer-facing mobile development (Android & iOS). Requires 8 years experience (Jay has 7) and 2 years of 0-to-1 product launches. Covers creator tools and platform experiences on YouTube.
roles.why_good_fit: Strong alignment with Jay's creator economy expertise — from founding a music streaming platform to creator monetisation research and content relevance work at a major social platform. Deep experience with consumer-facing mobile products serving Indian creators and regional/vernacular user segments. Proven 0-to-1 track record as a founding team member of a music streaming app. YouTube Creation touches creators, India market, and consumer product — three of Jay's strongest domains.
roles.how_to_win: Lead with the Resso founding story (0-to-1, creator-centric product, scaled to market leadership in India) and creator monetisation research that shaped strategic direction at a major short-video platform. Highlight GenZ Product Council membership and deep understanding of Indian creator behaviours. Address the 8-year requirement gap by emphasising breadth: founding team experience + startup founder + current role at a top-tier social company. Proactively acknowledge the mobile development specificity gap and reframe around product intuition for creator tools and platform growth.
roles.job_description: Senior Product Manager, YouTube, Creation and Platform. 8 years experience in PM or related technical role for consumer-facing mobile development on Android and iOS. 2 years 0-to-1 experience.

roles.title: Product Manager, YouTube Live Living Room
roles.role_summary: PM for YouTube's live streaming experience on TV/living room devices — a consumer video product at the intersection of live content and the connected TV surface.
roles.why_good_fit: Jay has direct live streaming and video product experience from ByteDance (Resso, TikTok) and Meta (Shoppertainment — live commerce). His understanding of Indian consumer video consumption patterns, regional content preferences, and GenZ viewing behaviours (GenZ Product Council) is highly relevant. The 3-year experience minimum is well below Jay's ~7 years, making him a strong senior candidate. His Resso experience — building a content consumption product from scratch with ML-driven recommendations — maps directly to understanding how users discover and engage with live content on TV.
roles.how_to_win: Frame your Resso story as evidence you understand content consumption product-market fit — you identified that 10% of content drove 90% of listening, then shaped the recommendation engine. Connect this to live content discovery on TV. Highlight Shoppertainment as proof you understand live streaming's commercial potential. Emphasize your field research skills — you can go into Indian living rooms and understand how families actually use YouTube on TV. Position the 3-year minimum as an opportunity for you to come in as a senior voice with 7 years of relevant experience.
roles.job_description: You'll own the YouTube Live experience on TV and living room devices — think smart TVs, streaming sticks, and gaming consoles. Day-to-day involves defining features that make live content more discoverable and engaging on the big screen, working with engineering and UX teams, and running experiments to improve viewer retention. Success means growing live viewership on TV surfaces and creating differentiated experiences that leverage the living room context.

roles.title: Product Marketing Manager, Google Search
roles.role_summary: Product Marketing Manager for Google Search — driving marketing strategy for Search products, likely with India market focus given the Gurugram location.
roles.why_good_fit: Jay has strong product-marketing adjacent skills: insight writing, competitive intelligence, stakeholder pitching, and market strategy. His India market intelligence is directly relevant for Search marketing in India. His understanding of Indian GenZ, regional users, and Tier 2/3 consumers maps to Search's India growth opportunity. The 6-year marketing experience requirement is a stretch — Jay's experience is product management, not marketing per se, though his work involves many marketing-adjacent activities (market research, user insights, competitive benchmarking, GTM strategy).
roles.how_to_win: Position your IPGS role at Meta as product marketing in practice — you wrote insights, built pitch decks, influenced product teams, and drove market strategy. Frame your competitive intelligence work (TikTok re-entry strategy) as marketing strategy. Highlight your understanding of how Indian users actually search — regional language patterns, voice-led behaviour, GenZ search habits. Acknowledge the marketing title is new but argue your skills are directly transferable. The Gurugram location is a concern for remote preference — clarify flexibility.
roles.job_description: You'll own product marketing strategy for Google Search — defining how Search products are positioned, launched, and grown in the market. Day-to-day involves developing marketing campaigns, working with product teams on launch strategy, analyzing market data, and driving user adoption. Success means growing Search usage and awareness through effective marketing.

roles.title: Product Marketing Manager, Google Play Core Growth
roles.role_summary: Product Marketing Manager for Google Play's core growth initiatives — driving user and developer growth for the Play ecosystem.
roles.why_good_fit: The 'Core Growth' mandate aligns with Jay's growth-oriented experience. His Resso scaling story (alpha → India's largest music streaming app) is directly relevant to platform growth. His understanding of Indian app ecosystem dynamics, Tier 2/3 user acquisition, and regional content preferences maps to Play's India growth opportunity. The 6-year marketing requirement is a stretch — same caveat as the Search PMM role. His cross-functional project management experience matches the requirement.
roles.how_to_win: Lead with Resso's growth story — you helped scale a platform from zero to India's #1 in its category. Frame your Meta IPGS work as growth marketing in practice. Highlight your understanding of what drives app adoption in India's Tier 2/3 markets. Emphasize your competitive intelligence skills (you monitored competitors daily at Meta) as relevant to growth strategy. Position yourself as someone who brings product intuition to marketing decisions.
roles.job_description: You'll own growth marketing strategy for Google Play — defining how to acquire and retain users and developers on the platform. Day-to-day involves developing growth campaigns, working with product teams on feature launches, analyzing growth metrics, and driving adoption. Success means growing Play's user base and developer ecosystem through effective marketing.

roles.title: mCommerce Marketing Manager, Apps Go-To-Market, Google Play
roles.role_summary: Marketing Manager for mobile commerce apps on Google Play — driving GTM strategy for mCommerce apps in the Play ecosystem.
roles.why_good_fit: Jay's Shoppertainment work at Meta (commerce + entertainment convergence) is directly relevant to mCommerce. His understanding of Indian e-commerce behaviour, SMB digital commerce patterns, and social commerce dynamics maps well. His experience with Indian consumer segments (Tier 2/3, GenZ) is valuable for mCommerce growth in India. The 6-year marketing requirement is a stretch, but his product and growth experience is transferable. His cross-functional project management experience matches.
roles.how_to_win: Lead with Shoppertainment — you helped define Meta India's biggest monetisation opportunity at the intersection of commerce and entertainment. Frame your SMB work as understanding how Indian merchants go digital. Highlight your understanding of Indian mCommerce dynamics — UPI adoption, social commerce, WhatsApp-based selling. Position yourself as someone who brings deep India commerce insights to Google Play's mCommerce strategy.
roles.job_description: You'll own GTM marketing strategy for mobile commerce apps on Google Play — helping mCommerce apps grow their presence and user base on the platform. Day-to-day involves developing marketing programs, working with app developers, analyzing marketplace data, and driving mCommerce app adoption. Success means growing the mCommerce app ecosystem on Google Play.

roles.title: Strategy and Operations Lead, GBO Tools
roles.role_summary: Strategy & Operations Lead for Google Business Operations tools — driving strategy and operational excellence for internal business tools.
roles.why_good_fit: Jay has 6+ years of experience matching the requirement. His strategic thinking (TikTok re-entry strategy at CPO level, Shoppertainment big bet identification) and operational execution (half planning, insight-to-execution methodology at Meta) are relevant. His AI fluency (personal AI OS, 80% task automation) could be valuable for tools strategy. However, this is an internal tools/operations role without a clear market growth or consumer product component, which limits the fit. It's a reasonable fallback if consumer PM roles don't materialize.
roles.how_to_win: Frame your Meta IPGS methodology as strategy & operations in practice — you built half plans, defined milestones, tracked execution, and drove cross-functional alignment. Highlight your AI OS as evidence you can think about tools strategy from a user perspective. Emphasize your consulting-like skills: opportunity sizing, business cases, stakeholder pitching. Position yourself as someone who brings product thinking to operations roles.
roles.job_description: You'll lead strategy and operations for Google's internal business operations tools — defining how tools are prioritized, developed, and adopted across the organization. Day-to-day involves strategic planning, stakeholder management, operational analysis, and driving tool adoption. Success means improving operational efficiency through better tools and processes.

roles.title: Senior Associate, Go-to-Market Business Strategy and Operations
roles.role_summary: Senior Associate in GTM Strategy & Operations — supporting go-to-market strategy for Google's business products, likely Ads or Cloud.
roles.why_good_fit: Jay's experience with GTM strategy (Shoppertainment, SMB advertiser conversion, MetaAI India growth) is relevant. His 4+ years of strategy experience matches the requirement. His India market intelligence could be valuable for GTM strategy in India. However, 'Senior Associate' may be a step down in seniority for someone with 7 years of experience, and the role may be more junior than Jay's target. The Gurugram location is also a concern for remote preference.
roles.how_to_win: Frame your Meta IPGS work as GTM strategy — you identified market opportunities, built business cases, and drove product execution. Highlight your Shoppertainment work as GTM strategy for a new monetisation vertical. Emphasize your analytical skills and ability to size opportunities. However, consider whether this role's seniority matches your career trajectory before investing application energy.
roles.job_description: You'll support go-to-market strategy and operations for Google's business products — analyzing market opportunities, building business cases, and driving operational execution. Day-to-day involves strategic analysis, cross-functional coordination, and operational planning. Success means effective GTM execution that drives business growth.

roles.title: Growth Manager
roles.role_summary: Growth Manager focused on digital advertising — driving advertiser growth and adoption of Google's advertising products.
roles.why_good_fit: Jay's SMB advertiser conversion work at Meta (converting active businesses to paying advertisers) is directly relevant. His 2.3% India revenue uplift from SMB enforcement work shows he can drive advertising revenue. His understanding of Indian SMBs and their advertising behaviour is valuable. The 5-year digital advertising requirement is a partial match — Jay has worked on advertising products at Meta but his primary role was product growth, not advertising sales. This is more of a sales/BD-adjacent role than a pure product role.
roles.how_to_win: Frame your Meta SMB work as advertiser growth — you helped convert active businesses to paying advertisers and drove 2.3% India revenue uplift. Highlight your understanding of Indian SMB advertising behaviour. Emphasize your product lens on growth — you don't just sell, you identify product opportunities that drive adoption. However, assess whether this is too sales-oriented for your career goals.
roles.job_description: You'll drive advertiser growth for Google's advertising products — helping businesses adopt and scale their use of Google Ads. Day-to-day involves consultative selling, analyzing advertiser data, developing growth strategies, and working with cross-functional teams. Success means growing advertiser spend and adoption.

roles.title: Core GTM Product Specialist, Google Play
roles.role_summary: GTM Product Specialist for Google Play, requiring 8 years in product management or product specialist roles in online commerce or content business. Bridges product and go-to-market for Google's app/content ecosystem.
roles.why_good_fit: Jay's experience spans content businesses (music streaming platform, short-video platform) and go-to-market strategy (Shoppertainment, SMB advertiser conversion). The role's focus on digital content commerce aligns with his background in content distribution, creator ecosystems, and Indian market growth. His methodology of translating market insights into product recommendations mirrors the GTM Product Specialist function.
roles.how_to_win: Position as someone who bridges product insight and market execution — cite the methodology of discovery → insight → pitch → execution partnership. Highlight experience in content businesses (music streaming, social media) and understanding of India's app/content ecosystem. Emphasise competitive intelligence capabilities and ability to work cross-functionally across product, marketing, and business teams.
roles.job_description: Core GTM Product Specialist, Google Play. 8 years in product management or product specialist role in online commerce or content business. E-commerce, gaming, or apps digital content experience.

roles.title: AI Deployment Engineer, Startups
roles.role_summary: AI Deployment Engineer role at OpenAI focused on startups, based remotely in India. Part of the Technical Success team — likely involves helping startups integrate and deploy OpenAI's products. Limited job description available.
roles.why_good_fit: OpenAI is Jay's top target company. Remote India is his P0 preference. The startup ecosystem focus aligns with his entrepreneurial background and understanding of Indian SMBs/startups. His top-percentile AI tool fluency and personal AI operating system demonstrate deep product-level understanding of AI deployment. However, the 'Engineer' title suggests a technical implementation role which may not be ideal.
roles.how_to_win: Worth investigating the full JD — the 'Technical Success' team framing may be more consultative/GTM than pure engineering. If so, Jay should lead with his AI fluency (top 5% usage at current company, personal AI OS, multi-agent workflow experience) and startup founder background. Highlight ability to understand startup needs from first principles and translate AI capabilities into business outcomes. Flag risk: if this is a pure coding/engineering role, it's a discard.
roles.job_description: AI Deployment Engineer, Startups. Technical Success team. India - Remote. Limited details available.

roles.title: AI Deployment Manager - India
roles.role_summary: Manages AI deployment and technical success for OpenAI's enterprise/startup customers in India — ensuring successful adoption and integration of OpenAI's APIs and products.
roles.why_good_fit: Jay has relevant experience working with SMBs and enterprises on AI adoption (MetaAI India field work covering enterprises, SMBs, and AI enthusiasts). His understanding of how Indian businesses actually operate (voice-led, informal digital literacy) is valuable for deployment strategy. His AI fluency is exceptional (top 5% Metamate user, personal AI OS with multiple agents). The India-remote setup is ideal. However, this is a Technical Success role — likely more customer-facing and implementation-focused than Jay's core product/growth skillset. His experience is more market intelligence and product strategy than deployment management.
roles.how_to_win: Lean into your understanding of how Indian businesses adopt technology — the SMB enforcement story shows you understand the gap between how global products assume Indian businesses work vs. reality. Highlight your AI OS as proof you can deeply understand deployment challenges. Position your field work with enterprises and SMBs during MetaAI India as direct experience understanding AI adoption barriers. Be transparent that your strength is the market intelligence and user understanding layer, and frame that as the missing piece in deployment success — technical deployment fails when you don't understand the user context.
roles.job_description: This role manages the deployment and adoption of OpenAI's AI products for customers in India. Day-to-day likely involves working with Indian enterprises and startups to successfully integrate OpenAI's APIs, troubleshooting adoption challenges, and ensuring customers achieve value from the products. Success looks like high customer satisfaction, successful deployments, and growing usage.

roles.title: Communications Manager, India
roles.role_summary: Manages OpenAI's public communications, media relations, and narrative in India — a PR/comms role rather than a product or growth role.
roles.why_good_fit: While Jay has strong writing skills (insight writing, competitive intelligence write-ups, strategic narratives) and deep India market context, this is fundamentally a communications/PR role. It lacks the product, growth, and strategy components that are core to Jay's experience and career trajectory. The India-remote setup is ideal, and OpenAI is a target company, but the role type doesn't leverage Jay's core strengths in product growth and market intelligence. His conciseness feedback area also works against a comms-heavy role.
roles.how_to_win: Not recommended as a primary application. If Jay decides to apply, he should frame his insight writing and stakeholder influence work at Meta as communications-adjacent — he's been shaping narratives about India for global product teams. But this would be a career pivot, not a natural progression.
roles.job_description: This role owns OpenAI's communications strategy in India — managing media relations, public narratives, crisis communications, and brand positioning. Day-to-day involves working with journalists, crafting press materials, managing public perception, and coordinating with global comms teams. Success looks like positive brand perception and effective narrative management in India.

roles.title: Consumer Marketing Manager, India
roles.role_summary: Leads consumer marketing for OpenAI's products (primarily ChatGPT) in India — driving awareness, adoption, and engagement through India-specific marketing strategies.
roles.why_good_fit: Jay's superpower is understanding Indian consumer segments deeply — GenZ (GenZ Product Council member, 25 globally), Tier 2/3 users (Resso thesis), regional/vernacular users, and SMBs. His MetaAI field work uncovered that Indian GenZ AI users prioritize language tone and persona over factual accuracy — a critical marketing insight for ChatGPT India positioning. His experience at Meta includes working with International Marketing teams on non-product lever execution. The Resso scaling story demonstrates he understands how to position and grow a consumer product in India against established competitors. Remote role in India checks his P0 requirement. While this is marketing-titled rather than PM, it has a strong product lens given it's about consumer AI adoption in India.
roles.how_to_win: Position yourself as someone who doesn't just market products but understands the Indian consumer psyche deeply enough to shape the marketing strategy from first principles. Lead with the MetaAI insight about language as a growth lever — this is a marketing goldmine for ChatGPT India. Share the Resso story: how you understood that Tier 2/3 Indian users needed a fundamentally different product positioning than what Spotify offered. Emphasize your GenZ Product Council membership and the nuanced Indian GenZ behaviours you've mapped. Frame your pitch: 'I know which Indian users will adopt ChatGPT next, why they'll adopt it, and what message will resonate — because I've spent years in the field with these exact users.'
roles.job_description: This role owns consumer marketing strategy for OpenAI in India — crafting campaigns, positioning, and go-to-market strategies that drive ChatGPT awareness and adoption among Indian consumers. Day-to-day involves market research, campaign development, cross-functional collaboration with product and growth teams, and measuring marketing effectiveness. Success looks like growing ChatGPT's consumer brand and user base in India.

roles.title: Growth, India
roles.role_summary: India growth role at OpenAI — likely focused on driving ChatGPT adoption, user acquisition, and market expansion across India's diverse user segments.
roles.why_good_fit: This is Jay's dream role. His entire career is a case study in India market growth for global tech platforms. At Meta, he drove 2.3% India revenue uplift from the SMB enforcement fix — the single largest revenue feature in Meta India history. He scaled Resso from alpha to India's largest music streaming app by targeting Tier 2/3 users that Spotify ignored. His MetaAI India field work (20+ user interviews) uncovered that language/tone matters more than factual correctness for Indian GenZ AI users — a direct growth lever for ChatGPT in India. His deep understanding of regional, vernacular, and GenZ Indian users, combined with AI fluency (top 5% Metamate user, personal AI OS), makes him uniquely positioned. He has zero-to-one experience (Resso founding team, travel startup), competitive intelligence depth (TikTok re-entry strategy reaching CPO level), and the rare ability to translate on-ground Indian observations into product decisions at scale.
roles.how_to_win: Lead with the narrative: 'I've spent 7 years figuring out how global tech products win in India — and I've done it at both ByteDance and Meta.' Open with the Dahod bakery → 10M businesses → 2.3% revenue uplift story as proof of ground-up India growth. Then pivot to the MetaAI India insight about language as a growth lever — this directly applies to ChatGPT's India opportunity. Emphasize the Resso story (beat Spotify in India by understanding Tier 2/3 users) as evidence of scaling a consumer product in India from zero. Highlight the AI OS you built on Metamate as proof of AI-native thinking. Frame your pitch around OpenAI's India opportunity: voice-first AI for regional users, vernacular ChatGPT adoption, SMB AI tools for India's informal economy. You understand these users because you've lived among them.
roles.job_description: This role likely owns OpenAI's India growth strategy — driving ChatGPT user acquisition, engagement, and retention across India's diverse user segments. Day-to-day involves identifying growth levers specific to the Indian market, running experiments, building local market intelligence, and partnering with product and engineering teams to adapt the product for India. Success looks like meaningful user growth metrics in India.
